preview_text:
The model favours Sweden to win at 40%, but the margin is modest; Tunisia's 29% underdog chance sits only 10 percentage points below. Neither team commands the match in probabilistic terms. The sportsbook consensus backs Sweden more decisively at 48%, a 9-point gap over the model's reading.

A draw at 31% emerges as the likeliest single outcome from the model's lens, narrowly outpacing a Swedish victory. The three-way spread is relatively compressed—no result carries dominant weight—suggesting the match will be genuinely competitive rather than tilted heavily toward the favourite. Tunisia's baseline case sits close enough to Sweden's that the outcome remains genuinely uncertain.

The model and market diverge on Sweden's win probability by roughly 8 percentage points, with the sportsbooks attributing higher confidence to a Swedish victory and lower odds to Tunisia. Both sources treat a draw as plausible but not the most likely scenario. The dashboard does not adjudicate which forecast is more accurate; the gap reflects genuine disagreement between statistical and market pricing.
